description_long: This bungalow features a single-story rectangular structure with a prominent red gable roof made of overlapping tiles. The front facade includes a covered porch supported by wooden posts, and three symmetrically arranged windows with brown shutters and flower boxes beneath them. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, contrasting with the dark base trim and the roof. On one side, a wooden staircase leads up to a side entrance, while the rear and opposite side are more plain, with minimal window openings. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on symmetry and practical outdoor access. The architectural style and materials suggest a cozy, suburban residential dwelling.
description: A single-story bungalow with a red gable roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical windows with brown shutters and flower boxes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, three windows with shutters and flower boxes, and the main entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the gable roof and the plain wall with no windows or doors.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full expanse of the red tiled roof and a plain back wall with a small staircase visible on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden staircase, a small window, and the side of the porch.
